change: Add rule: doc-discipline (classify-before-write; one home per type; pointer-only index)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; approved by decision

change: Adopt vocabulary (docs/VOCABULARY.md), data-layer/traceability model (governance/design/data-layer.md), agent definition (docs/definitions/agent.md), and docs index
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user vocabulary spec + recovered agent roster from archive/os-v1

change: Declare write-zones (path authority): governance/design/write-zones.md + governance/permissions.json
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; write-zones not branches; enforcement via write-fence (Stage 2) + CODEOWNERS (active)

change: Add rule: branching (branch owns a work-item 1:1; main protected; PR-only; SessionStart binding fail-closed; enforcement Stage 2/3)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; approved

change: Encode governance-vs-docs rule (normative=governance / descriptive=docs, one authority) into doc-discipline; rename governance/design->governance/decisions and docs/design->docs/architecture
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; approved

change: Capture founding principles (docs/principles.md): 1) Bound to be free; 2) Observability is the precondition for governance
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; mayor/jurisdiction + bounds-enable-freedom

change: Document the governing structure (define before build): governance/decisions/component-model.md + governance/decisions/authority.md
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; v1 lesson = build-before-define caused sprawl; document first

change: Add governance readiness checklist (governance/readiness.md): the single 'is the governance layer in place?' view, Defined vs Enforced, governance-layer vs operational-layer split
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; need to see governance state at a glance and gate the operational layer on it

change: Add machine-readable environment registry (governance/environment.json) + live audit snapshot in CONTROLS-REGISTER: this OS v2 container has no repo hooks; only two host-injected hooks run; legacy local hooks do not fire here
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user asked three times for clarity on what hooks/controls are running

change: Tighten 'enforced' in readiness.md: add enforcement ladder (per-run/gated-at-merge/on-demand/by-hand); honest re-grade — nothing is per-run enforced, strongest tier is gated-at-merge; add in/out-of-harness classification rule (declaration=around, enforcing script=in)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: 'enforced means every single run'; classify env registry vs its future audit script

change: Define the Governability Test (governance/decisions/governability.md): a rule is governable iff Bound (condition, or judgement with basis+acceptance) + Observable + Triggerable + Clear Exit = the four contract facets as an acceptance test. Wired as the entry test for readiness.md
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user named the test of whether a principle/rule is governable

change: Make branching phased: BOOTSTRAP = single branch main, direct push, no new branches, enforced by a GitHub ruleset (Restrict creations, all branches, admin bypass). Operational PR-per-work-item model goes dormant until the first non-human writer / operational wiring. Documented-only branching didn't bind (branches got created) -> enforcement moved to the platform
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: 'create a rule to ensure no new branches are created for now' + relaxed require-PR for direct-to-main

change: Branching enforcement finding: ruleset bypass list must be EMPTY, not 'Repository admin'. The session pushes authenticated as the repo admin (jeremy-james-state via the GitHub App), so an admin bypass exempts the very actor being bound — test branch pushed through. Principle: a control that exempts the identity the agent uses is not a control. Escape hatch = disable/re-enable the ruleset, not a standing bypass
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; live test of the ruleset

change: Add 5th governability criterion: Tested/Verified (four declared, one demonstrated) — a rule is not a rail until watched to bind both ways (rejects + allows). Worked example: bootstrap-no-new-branches ruleset tested live (new branch -> GH013 rejected; main push allowed). Updated readiness entry-test + G12 = first per-run-enforced, tested control
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: 'governable should also be tested... + Tested/Verified'; live test of the ruleset

change: Reverse bootstrap no-new-branches model; adopt Model A (GitHub standard): main protected, no direct push, short-lived work/<id> branch -> PR -> required doctor check -> merge -> auto-delete. Reason: a server required-check cannot gate a direct push (only a PR), and a local pre-push hook is not standard governance (bypassable, unlogged)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user chose Option A; GitHub standard best practice = PR + required status checks

change: Add explicit branch deletion rules: merged -> auto-deleted (repo setting 'Automatically delete head branches'); PR-closed -> deleted; straggler-no-PR -> deleted by hygiene audit; main never deleted. Sprawl is controlled by deleting merged branches, not forbidding branches
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: 'Option A with clear branch deletion rules'

change: Define the identity model (governance/decisions/identity.md): three distinct identities — human governor (admin+reviewer), Claude actor (scoped non-admin), CI doctor (checker). Standard for an automated actor = GitHub App (claude[bot]); but Claude Code on the web acts as the connecting account, so interactive sessions need a dedicated non-admin Claude machine account for attribution. Closes the bypass gap (agent must not be admin). Wiring is the human's (session can't switch its own auth)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user wants GitHub activity attributed to Claude not the human; researched code.claude.com web auth + claude-code-action custom App

change: Revise identity model: GitHub App is PRIMARY (was hybrid); machine account demoted to fallback. The App is the single governed write-identity for the whole harness: capability = a scoped short-lived token (create-github-app-token), per-agent/per-run, = GitHub-layer enforcement of write-zones + authority. Web session plans/triggers; App writes as claude[bot] via Actions; human stays admin+reviewer
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: GitHub App is more logical + it lets us configure how harness agents write to the repo (big long-run payoff)

change: Decision: governance governs itself — the governing layer is held to the same rigor it imposes. Gap: the doctor skips governance/ (KNOWN_NON_HARNESS), so governance is gated only by process, not content. Rule: a governance check (sibling of the doctor) validates governance/ at the same CI gate (no orphans, rules pass governability test, ledger integrity, permissions<->write-zones consistency, cross-refs resolve, classification holds). Mechanism planned, not built
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: governance has its own codebase and needs the same level of rigor as the harness; the binder must be bound

change: Add rationale to identity.md: author vs actor (commits show Claude; actor shows the human because the session acts with the human's access); the web 'tool extends you' assistant model vs the App 'autonomous bounded actor' model; Anthropic ships both, a harness needs the second; default is a connection choice not a wall. Records the reasoning behind choosing the App write-path
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user questioned why the Claude web app acts on-behalf-of and 'looks like you wrote it' — logged the why

change: Build harness/governance-check.mjs and wire it into CI: validate governance ledger JSONL integrity, resolve governance markdown links, warn on orphan decisions/rules, and fail on write-zone owner conflicts. Add focused node:test coverage and index the governance docs so the self-governance check has live references to verify.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user requested the governance doctor as a sibling of harness/doctor.mjs at the same merge gate

change: Relocate doctor + governance-check (and tests) into governance/enforcement, rewire CI/router/manifest references, and add governance/enforcement/structure-check.mjs (WARN-only top-level schema drift check).
basis: Phase 3 repo-structure decision (governance-as-catch-all, enforcement lives inside governance) approved in this change

change: Declare .claude/ as a recognized top-level folder (tier: tooling/config, Claude Code session commands); add /handoff slash command; refresh docs/RESUME-HERE.md to clean placeholder
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; session-handoff capability reinstated; .claude/ is NOT part of the operational harness

change: Activate the main branch-protection ruleset and flip governance/rules/branching.md Status from Declared to Enforced. Ruleset (bypass list EMPTY): require a pull request with 0 approvals, require the `doctor` status check (strict / branch up-to-date), block force pushes; repo setting auto-delete head branches ON. The empty bypass list is load-bearing per gov-2026-06-30-013 (a control that exempts the agent's admin identity is not a control). Per gov-2026-06-30-014 a rule is not a rail until watched to bind both ways: this PR proves the allow-path (doctor gates, passing merge allowed, head branch auto-deletes); the reject-path (direct push to main refused) was demonstrated live earlier and is re-confirmed by the standing ruleset.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; jeremy configured the ruleset in the GitHub UI and confirmed bypass empty + doctor required + require-PR/0-approvals + block force-push + auto-delete; recorded as the first work/<id> PR, which doubles as the enforcement proof

change: Add rule harness-admission (Located·Registered·Contracted·Green·Admitted) + register the signal-ledger CANDIDATE in governance/candidates.md and build it in harness/sandbox/ (contract + ledger + intake + view + tests, green); wire it per-turn via tracked .claude/settings.json. Candidate only — NOT admitted, NOT in manifest.json, NOT in harness/core. First piece of the data layer: every run becomes a four-tuple-stamped signal in record/signals.jsonl (truth), projected into state/os.db (rebuildable).
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: signals=every input, four-tuple=traceability, must fire on every run; admission rule approved (A.Yes); candidate registered lightweight + located in sandbox per user choice

change: Wire the signal-ledger candidate to a hosted projection: add Supabase os_signals table (RLS on, realtime enabled) in the work-tracker project, a sync.mjs that upserts record/signals.jsonl→Supabase (idempotent on n), and .github/workflows/sync-signals.yml that runs the sync in CI on push (the Claude container cannot reach Supabase directly). DATA EGRESS: full signal incl. summary text now leaves the repo to a hosted DB. Candidate wiring only — NOT admitted, NOT in manifest.json.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user chose option 1 (wire live sync + realtime); Supabase=store/Vercel=UI split; full-signal egress approved

change: Automate the capture loop: intake.mjs now commits + pushes record/signals.jsonl after appending (new persist.mjs commitAndPush, stages only the signal path, fail-open). Makes the signal loop hands-off — clean working tree (no Stop-hook nag) and the push triggers the CI→Supabase sync with no manual step. Also added workflow_dispatch to sync-signals.yml for on-demand verification. Candidate wiring only — NOT admitted.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: automate the commit and push (gut feel) so the loop is hands-off

change: Add intake filter (filter.mjs): only REAL inputs become signals — drop task-notifications, system-reminders, Stop-hook feedback, webhook/untrusted envelopes — so the auto-committing loop stops recording noise. Document the signal loop placement + roadmap (docs/architecture/signal-loop.md): signal-ledger home = the data layer signal store + extractor; Supabase = a hosted projection IN the data layer; received-now / sent-later (Stop hook, linked by run); staged extract→classify→route→act with one-candidate-per-stage admission to prevent v1 sprawl. Define-before-build. Candidate only — NOT admitted.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: drop system turns; document the home before building further; preserve governance discipline + avoid the v1 sprawl; signal extractor/dispatch is the next interesting piece

change: Decision (B): keep record/signals.jsonl TRACKED in the signal-ledger candidate — a deliberate, time-boxed divergence from main PR#23 policy (record/ runtime streams gitignored, durable home = Data Layer; signals pre-reserved gitignored). Justification: sandbox-only candidate (NOT admitted); the container cannot reach Supabase directly, so git push→CI→Supabase is the only working transport to the Data Layer today (git is courier, not home); reverts to gitignored when the Data Layer is formed. Scoped to one file on this branch; main policy unchanged for all other streams. Recorded so the divergence is owned, not silent drift.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user chose Option B (keep mechanism) with clear justification, sandbox experiment; main advanced via #22/#23/#25 during this branch life

change: Govern CI workflows as controls (re-applied on a clean branch after the #24 squash did not carry it). Rule governance/rules/ci-workflows.md: a workflow runs code/holds secrets/egresses data → must be declared in environment.json + ledgered + owner-approved; undeclared = drift. Declared the two undeclared workflows in environment.json L3_repo.controls: claude.yml (claude[bot] write-path) and sync-signals.yml (signals→Supabase, full-signal egress). Added checkDeclaredWorkflows to governance-check.mjs — fail-closed ERROR on undeclared workflow at the merge gate (+ tests).
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user flagged the sync check arriving without explicit governance; chose Govern additions (rule + declare); re-applied after squash omission

change: Retire the auto-commit hook; re-align signals with main record/ policy. intake.mjs is now CAPTURE-ONLY (append the signal, no commit/push); removed persist.mjs + its test. Untracked record/signals.jsonl (now gitignored per PR#23 runtime-streams policy), ending the gov-029 tracked-signals divergence. Reason: once the auto-commit/push hook reached main it caused repo-wide churn — a commit+push every turn, every session, polluting branches/PRs and attempting pushes to protected main. Consequence: capture is local-only; reaching the Data Layer (Supabase) is deferred to Data Layer formation; sync.mjs + sync-signals.yml marked dormant.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: address the auto-commit hook (root cause of churn); approved capture-only + gitignore signals, sync paused

change: Start the work-in-progress data layer (human form): record/incidents/ — a tracked-markdown incident log with the 5-step resolution rule (Root cause / Recreated / Immediate fix / Long-term / Preventative) brought forward from the archived method, plus a _template.md and a README. Filed the first incident (auto-commit-hook churn). Incidents are filed BY HAND for now; automated routing is the orchestrator/dispatcher future job; the machine incidents stream (gitignored jsonl -> Data Layer) is the later automated form.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: create a WIP markdown data layer (folders of markdown), file incidents by hand as they arise, ring-fenced governance without letting agents loose

change: Make the incident log deterministic: add harness/sandbox/incident/incident.mjs (candidate) — STEPS + missingSteps/isComplete brought forward from the archived incident.mjs, adapted from SQLite to the markdown data layer, plus a new/check/list CLI (check exits non-zero on a resolved-but-incomplete incident). Add the /incident slash command (.claude/commands/incident.md) that drives it: the script guarantees the 5-step structure + completeness, the human/agent fills the content. First brick of the deterministic layer.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: turn incident.mjs into a /incident command, deterministic; start forming the deterministic harness layer

change: Define the FIRST agent — the Investigator (candidate). It is the roster Investigator (owns what information best reduces uncertainty?), applied to incidents, NOT a new agent (agent.md: do not fold). Two-part structure per agent.md: definition (the law) at governance/agents/investigator.md + candidate code-side at harness/sandbox/investigator/ (contract.json + README). Rewired the /incident command to DISPATCH the Investigator as a bounded subagent that investigates + fills the incident (human no longer writes it); exit = incident.mjs isComplete; it writes exactly one incident file, never fixes/merges. Contained per the governability test. When an orchestrator exists it moves to harness/runners/investigator/.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: /incident should dispatch an LLM to do the work, not the human; first contained agent self-deployed by the command; grounded in the repo agent/runner definitions; it can be the Investigator

change: Capture an OPEN decision-stub: platform-home (governance/decisions/platform-home.md). Question: is GitHub the long-term home for the whole harness? Framing: GitHub excels as the governance backbone (versioned truth, the required-check merge gate, branch-protection rulesets, the App identity) but strains as a runtime/data layer (the session evidence: auto-commit churn, the #24 squash clobber amid concurrent merges, gitignored runtime streams, the container cannot reach Supabase, concurrent-merge coordination). Tentative direction (NOT decided): GitHub stays the home for law+record+gate; the runtime + Data Layer get their own home (compute + DB), connected. Deciding that = forming the Data Layer. Recorded now, decided deliberately later. (Note: ids gov-033/034 are reserved by in-flight PRs #31/#32; this uses 035.)
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user asked whether GitHub is the long-term home; every session pain was GitHub-as-runtime, every smooth thing was GitHub-as-governance

change: Fix + log the incident-command synchronous-block issue. The /incident command dispatched the Investigator inline, blocking the main channel for the whole investigation (~a minute) — caught on first real use. Fix: /incident now dispatches the Investigator as a BACKGROUND subagent (run_in_background) and returns immediately, reporting back on completion; the Investigator definition records execution = off-the-main-channel (never inline). Filed the incident (resolved, all five steps) at record/incidents/incident-2026-06-30-incident-command-blocks-the-main-channel-synchronously.md — the log first self-caught design issue. Candidate wiring only; not admitted.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; /incident hung the main channel on first use; user: fix it (async) and file the incident; both

change: Make the Investigator self-contained so a completed background investigation does not bounce chores back to the main channel: it now self-verifies (incident.mjs check), appends its own log-index line to record/incidents/README.md, and reports a one-line result; the /incident command completion step becomes a one-line relay. Deliberate widening of the write-bound from exactly-one-incident-file to within-record/incidents/-only (its incident file + its log line) — recorded as a trade-off vs the Copilot #32 containment note. Updated the command, investigator.md, contract.json. Folded into PR #34.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: self-contained lifecycle — agent does its own verify + log-update + one-line report, cut the main-channel chores

change: Land the manifest/registry-split ADR (governance/decisions/manifest-registry.md): registry.json = component rows, manifest.json = rails, merged by the doctor/renderer; per-component contract.json deferred while planned; records the OPEN provisional question of harness shape (reshape folders to mirror the data model vs keep type-folders + improve instances/executionContexts + versions). Indexed in decisions/README, cross-linked from component-model. Recovered from the orphaned claude/registry-split branch (commit bf8c917, session ...013e1i) which was never merged after PR #27; renumbered from its colliding gov-030 to gov-038.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; branch-cleanup inspection found the unmerged ADR worth landing; split shipped in PR #27; ledger id renumbered to avoid the gov-030 collision on main

change: Reinstate /handoff and store handoffs in the markdown data layer (record/handoffs/), mirroring record/incidents/. New tracked-markdown folder: README (area + two surfaces: dated history files + the latest render) and _template.md (the fixed seven sections — Current state / Mission / Working model / What's on main / In flight / Next steps / Gotchas). /handoff now writes a dated history file record/handoffs/handoff-<date>.md (append-style) plus overwrites docs/RESUME-HERE.md as the latest pointer; it no longer writes record/handoffs.jsonl (gitignored, would not persist). Filed the first handoff (handoff-2026-06-30.md). Updated record/README + record/SCHEMA to record incidents+handoffs as the interim tracked-markdown human form of those streams. Closes the persistence gap the previous RESUME-HERE flagged.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: reinstate the handoff workflow/agent and store the handoff in the temporary data layer — the folder with MD files

change: Name what the harness IS: governance + exactly two enforcement points (one gate per plane). New decision governance/decisions/enforcement-points.md: Gate #1 = the commit gate (doctor-green + merge, control plane, BUILT, merge-blocking) — admission folds into it; Gate #2 = the table-scoped write hooks (execution plane, decides which store/agent may write which os.db table at write time, SPECIFIED but NOT BUILT — today only a static gate-time, path-level declaration exists via permissions.json + governance-check one-owner-per-write-zone; the runtime os.db-table hook lands with the data-layer stores run-store/work-store). Corollary: harness/runners/, state/, record/ are substrate the harness operates on, not the harness. Sharpened component-model.md (the executing code is substrate; the contract+registry+enforcement that binds it is the harness). Indexed in decisions/README.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user definition: strip away substrate and the harness reduces to a set of rules and the two points where they are enforced (commit gate + write hooks); harness = governance + its enforcement points; user: write it up

change: Lock the layered architecture (capstone) and close the three version/schema gaps. New decision governance/decisions/layered-architecture.md: three layers (governance=definition, execution=running instance/pinned checkout + orchestrator loop, data=tables), two gates (commit gate + write hooks, one per layer boundary), one loop (extract->classify->route->act->outcome->reconcile); Definition->Instance->Substrate; contracts authored-up/projected-down (truth->projection law); versioning on two axes (manifestVersion=shape, harnessVersion=content) with the running instance pinning a version and every runtime row stamped with the version it ran under. Indexed at the top of decisions/README. Closes: (1) added harness/contract.schema.json (schemas the third part of the definition; backs Gate #2's reads/writes); (2) the doctor now ENFORCES the schemas via governance/enforcement/schema-validate.mjs (zero-dep JSON-Schema subset) + checkSchemas — manifest/registry/contracts validated, violation=ERROR (the live definition validates clean); (3) the signals stream now stamps harnessVersion + codeSha on every row (versioning meets traceability), with the run-store/work-store to inherit it. +9 tests (schema-validate + doctor checkSchemas + ledger version-stamp). Candidate wiring only for the data-layer parts; not admitted.
basis: conversation 2026-06-30; user: write up the capstone layered-architecture decision plus close the three gaps (contract schema, doctor validates against schemas, runtime rows stamped with the harness version)

change: Environmental-control finding + decisions. The confinement PreToolUse fence (harness/sandbox/confinement) does NOT confine writes to os-rebuild: adversarial probe of decide() showed 8/10 outside-writes escape — fail-OPEN on any error (garbage input -> exit 0); it is a sibling-~/Projects blocklist, not an os-rebuild allowlist, so /tmp, home root, ~/.claude, /etc are writable; Bash $VAR/$HOME/cd-chain bypass the substring regex; MCP write tools uncovered; lexical resolve() -> symlink escape; root-scoped only. Decided: (a) NEW WORKSTREAM to harden via a kernel/OS sandbox (sandbox-exec/.sb, mirroring the Core claude-safe/harness.sb) + a fail-closed hook redesign (allowlist, realpath, all write tools incl MCP) — spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-confinement-sandbox-design.md; (b) adopt the BINDING PRINCIPLE 'controls fail closed, observability fails open' (harness-version-control spec section 12.1). Note: confinement is currently UNDECLARED in environment.json, and environment.json reflects the cloud container not this local context — declaring the local control properly is a follow-up tied to executionContexts.
scope: harness/sandbox/confinement/index.mjs (finding), docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-confinement-sandbox-design.md (new), docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-harness-version-control-design.md (section 12.1), governance/environment.json (undeclared control noted, declaration deferred)
basis: conversation 2026-07-01; user asked whether writes are truly confined to os-rebuild; adversarial evidence gathered live; user directed workstream (2) + principle (3) + logging this as an environmental change in-repo
